title: UX/UI Designer
description:
**This is Worldline** 




 Help shape the future of how the world pays and gets paid. At Worldline, we enable businesses of all sizes to grow quickly, simply, and securely. As a leader in the payments industry, we power millions of companies across five continents. Just as we drive our customers' growth, we support our people in building their careers. Together, we shape the evolution of payments.
 



  





 If you're passionate about designing intuitive, beautifully crafted solutions and eager to grow alongside a talented team, this is your chance. Join us and help redefine how the world pays.
 



  





**The opportunity** 




 Design at Worldline is going through an exciting transformation. We're raising the bar — fast. We're building a world-class design system, embedding user-centered thinking deeper into how products are shaped, and growing a diverse, talented team that is genuinely changing how the organisation approaches design. The momentum is real, the ambition is high, and the impact is visible.
 



  





 As a Product Designer in the Worldline Design team, you will play a crucial role in shaping our product portfolio — from the visual identity of our interfaces to the end-to-end experience our customers have with our solutions. Your mission is to advocate for the user's needs while elevating the visual quality and coherence of our products, defining and implementing intuitive solutions for the product direction, visual design, and interaction to enhance the overall user journey. You will bring a keen eye for aesthetics and a strong understanding of user-centered design, combining visual craft with thoughtful interaction design to create experiences that are both beautiful and functional.
 



  





 This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden.
 



  





**Day-to-day responsibilities** 



* Design visually polished, intuitive interfaces for complex payment solutions — bringing both aesthetic quality and usability together.
* Shape the visual language of our products — contributing to and evolving typography, colour, spacing, iconography, and layout across our portfolio.
* Collaborate with our Design System experts to grow and improve our world-class Design System, ensuring visual consistency and component quality.
* Transform business needs into clear, compelling designs and interactions with attention to visual detail and craft.
* Create high-fidelity UI designs and interactive prototypes that communicate design intent clearly to developers and stakeholders.
* Participate in design workshops and user testing to validate and refine your work together with colleagues.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams — including product managers, developers, and researchers — to deliver a polished, cohesive user experience.
* Stay ahead of visual design and industry trends, bringing fresh ideas and inspiration that elevate our standards.
* Actively explore opportunities for our business and the field of UX with generative and Agentic AI.
* Validate your designs with real users to measure impact and continuously seek opportunities to optimise and improve their experience.
* Become an active member of the Worldline Design team, contributing to our supportive design culture, critique practices, and shared knowledge.



  





**Who are we looking for** 




 We look for talented, thoughtful designers who care about their craft. People who bring fresh perspectives, step up and show what's next — optimistic, upbeat people with a can-do attitude, a hunger to learn and grow, and a genuine love for collaboration. In practice this means:
 


* 3+ years of experience in product design, with strong skills in both visual/UI design and interaction design.
* A keen eye for visual detail — you care about typography, colour, spacing, hierarchy, and the nuances that elevate an interface from functional to delightful.
* Experience working with and contributing to design systems, with an understanding of how visual consistency scales across products.
* Experience working with scrum teams and excellent skills in Figma and other design tools, including high-fidelity UI design and prototyping.
* Strong analytical skills — you get to the bottom of things to understand core problems, not just the surface level.
* Knowledge of user research practices and experience using usability testing platforms.
* Strong verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to present your design thinking clearly to audiences with varying levels of design understanding.
* Deep empathy for users and a passion for representing their needs and motivations to diverse stakeholder groups.
* Creative problem-solving skills and a thoughtful, proactive mindset in identifying opportunities for improving the user experience.
* An optimistic, collaborative personality — you bring warmth and energy to the team, value diverse perspectives, and believe the best work happens when people support each other.
* Experience in B2B SaaS and/or the Payments sector is a nice-to-have — strong design skills are what matter most, and industry knowledge you'll pick up on the job.
* You have an online portfolio showcasing your visual and product design work, which you can share upon application.
* You are available to work full time.



  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Worldline you'll get the chance to be at the heart of the global payments technology industry and shape how the world pays and gets paid. On top of that, you will also:
 


* Work with inspiring colleagues in a supportive, inclusive environment and be empowered to learn, grow and accelerate your career.
* At Worldline our top priority is to engage, encourage and develop you to help you improve your potential. We have comprehensive training and development programs in place demonstrating our dedication to developing your career.
* Health contribution
* Health check – Every 2:nd year
* Private Health Care Insurance (EuroAccident)
* Parental pay and 12 months pension during parental leave
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard
* Benefit portal with many great offers, plus more.



  





**Shape the evolution.** 




 We are pushing towards the next frontiers of payments technology, and we look for talented people to join our journey. People with passion, a can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. Here you'll work with ambitious colleagues from around the world, take on unique challenges as a team, and make a real impact on society. And with our empowering culture, strong technology and extensive training opportunities, we help you accelerate your career. Wherever you decide to go. Join our global team of over 18,000 innovators across 40+ countries, and shape a tomorrow that is yours to own.
 



  





 Learn more about life at Worldline at jobs.worldline.com
 



  





 We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or any applicable legally protected characteristics.

title: Junior designer
description:
Position: Junior Designer
   

  

 Agency: Essen
   

  

 Location: Stockholm
   

  

 Deadline: 31/03/2026
   

  

 Contract: Full-time
   

  

 Level: Junior
   

  

 We’re growing our Stockholm team and looking for designers who cares deeply about the work.
   

  

 You’re ambitious about your craft and want to get better, year after year. You’re looking for a place that will challenge you, back you, and expect a lot from you.
   

  

 You’re curious. You test ideas. You push them further. And you know that good design is rarely an accident.
   

  

 You’ll be part of shaping concepts together with strategists, writers and clients. We take ideas seriously. And we enjoy the process.
   

  

 Your profile
   

  

 You have a solid foundation in graphic design and branding. You understand typography, composition, systems and how ideas translate across touchpoints.
   

  

 You generate ideas easily, but you also care about kerning, grids and the final file.
   

  

 You aim for high standards in both thinking and craft. You can zoom out to see the strategic direction, and zoom in to refine the details.
   

  

 You like working with people who challenge you. And you’re generous, grounded and straightforward to work with.
   

  

 What we expect from you
   

  

* Proven competencies within design discipline
* Expert level skills in typical design softwares (Adobe Creative Suite and Figma)
* Conceptual and strategic thinker
* Understands the value and importance of design in modern businesses
* Can thrive in a high paced environment


 What we offer you
   

  

* Working in an international environment, with global brands
* Professional development through our educational fund
* Full insurance benefits
* Wellness benefits

title: Junior Web Designer
description:
The Junior Web Designer will support our team as we provide advisory services to a Swedish infrastructure group. This role focuses on creating clear digital interfaces that help translate complex financial findings into accessible visual tools for our clients.
   

  

**About Blue Oak Consulting**
 Blue Oak Consulting provides independent advisory on pricing, portfolio economics, capital allocation, and acquisition assumptions. We are not a traditional consulting firm focused on broad transformation programs or high level strategy decks. Instead, we interrogate specific commercial decisions where the financial stakes are high and the existing narrative is not sufficient. Our team looks past corporate language and P&L complexity to identify where value is leaking or capital is trapped. We work across sectors like software, manufacturing, and healthcare to test whether the assumptions behind a growth plan or an acquisition actually hold under pressure. We are a small, principal led firm where the people who do the work are the ones accountable for the results. We do not take transaction contingent fees, which allows us to be completely honest about whether the economics of a decision make sense. Our goal is to provide clarity through financial discipline and a healthy amount of skepticism.
   

  

**The Role**
 Working as a Junior Web Designer at Blue Oak Consulting is different from working at a creative agency. You will not be building flashy marketing sites: you will be designing the tools and interfaces that our clients use to understand their own economics. This is a fully remote position that requires a disciplined approach to work and a high degree of self organization. You will work closely with our senior advisors to turn raw data and financial models into functional web based dashboards and layouts. The daily environment is focused on precision and clarity over decoration. Because we are a small firm, you will see exactly how your designs contribute to the decisions our clients make.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing** 
* Designing and maintaining user interfaces for internal pricing tools and client facing financial dashboards.
* Translating complex data sets and spreadsheet outputs into clean, intuitive web layouts.
* Creating wireframes and interactive prototypes to test how users interact with financial information.
* Updating and managing visual assets across our digital platforms to ensure a professional and consistent appearance.
* Collaborating with senior consultants to understand how design choices can improve the clarity of a financial argument.
* Testing site functionality and layout consistency across various browsers and devices.
* Building simple web components using standard HTML and CSS to support our reporting tools.
* Reviewing existing layouts to identify areas where visual clutter can be reduced to improve user focus.
  

  


**What You Bring** 
* A degree or recent coursework in web design, graphic design, or a related field.
* A portfolio that demonstrates a strong understanding of layout, typography, and user interface principles.
* Familiarity with design tools such as Figma, Adobe XD, or Sketch.
* Basic knowledge of HTML and CSS and a general understanding of how designs are implemented on the web.
* A curious mindset and an interest in how people use data to make business decisions.
* Careful attention to detail, specifically regarding alignment, spacing, and font consistency.
* The ability to explain design choices using logic and evidence rather than personal preference.
* Willingness to learn the basics of financial reporting to better inform your design decisions.
  

  


**What We Offer** 
* Competitive pay that is attractive for an entry level position in the current market.
* A fully remote work environment that allows for a high degree of autonomy and flexibility.
* Direct access to the firm principals, providing an opportunity to learn about high level financial advisory.
* Hands on experience working on high stakes projects, such as our current engagement with a Swedish infrastructure group.
* A culture that values evidence and directness over corporate politics and hierarchy.
* The chance to build a portfolio of work that focuses on functional, data driven design.
  

  


 At Blue Oak Consulting, we believe that the best work comes from small teams of people who are directly responsible for their results. We avoid the overhead and layers typical of larger firms, which means our junior staff are involved in substantive work early on. We look for individuals who prefer clarity to complexity and who are comfortable asking the questions others might overlook. This role provides a foundation in both professional web design and the practical application of financial discipline in a remote setting.

 Saab is a leading defence and security company with an enduring mission, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 26,100 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.
   

  

 Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defence capability of several nations. Read more about us here
   

  

 Do you want to get to know our business and our people a little bit better? Here you can find some stories told by our employees.
   

  

*Kindly observe that this is an ongoing recruitment process and that the position might be filled before the closing date of the advertisement.*

title: UX Designer Mobile App
description:
**This is Worldline** 




 Help shape the future of how the world pays and gets paid. At Worldline, we enable businesses of all sizes to grow quickly, simply, and securely. As a leader in the payments industry, we power millions of companies across five continents. Just as we drive our customers' growth, we support our people in building their careers. Together, we shape the evolution of payments.
 



 If you're passionate about designing intuitive mobile experiences and eager to shape how merchants manage their business on the go, this is your chance. Join us and help redefine how the world pays.
 



  





**The opportunity** 




 Design at Worldline is going through an exciting transformation. We're raising the bar — fast. We're building a world-class design system, embedding user-centered thinking deeper into how products are shaped, and growing a diverse, talented team that is genuinely changing how the organisation approaches design. The momentum is real, the ambition is high, and the impact is visible.
 



  





 As a Product Designer with a mobile focus in the Worldline Design team, you will play a crucial role in shaping our mobile product experiences — the way our customers interact with our solutions on the go. Your mission is to advocate for the user's needs, defining and implementing intuitive solutions for the product direction, visuals, and experience to enhance their overall mobile user journey. You will bring deep expertise in native mobile design, leveraging your understanding of platform conventions, touch-first interactions, and on-the-go usage contexts to craft experiences that feel natural and effortless. While your primary focus will be on mobile, you will also contribute to the broader product experience, ensuring seamless continuity across platforms.
 



  





 This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden.
 



  





**Day-to-day responsibilities** 



* Design intuitive mobile app interfaces for Worldline's payment solutions, delivering experiences that delight users across iOS and Android platforms.
* Champion mobile-specific design patterns, including touch interactions, gestures, responsive layouts, and platform-specific conventions (Human Interface Guidelines & Material Design).
* Collaborate with our Design System experts to grow and improve our world-class Design System, ensuring scalability and consistency across mobile platforms.
* Transform business needs into clear, compelling mobile designs, interactions, and micro-animations.
* Design and prototype end-to-end mobile flows, considering connectivity states, performance constraints, and on-the-go usage contexts.
* Participate in design workshops and mobile usability testing to validate and refine your work together with colleagues.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams — including mobile developers, product managers, and researchers — to deliver a polished, cohesive user experience.
* Stay ahead of mobile industry trends, bringing fresh ideas and inspiration that elevate our standards.
* Actively explore opportunities for our business and the field of UX with generative and Agentic AI, particularly in mobile contexts.
* Validate your designs with real users to measure impact and continuously seek opportunities to optimise and improve their mobile experience.
* Become an active member of the Worldline Design team, contributing your mobile expertise and learning from colleagues across the broader design organisation.



  





**Who are we looking for** 




 We look for talented, thoughtful designers who care about their craft. People who bring fresh perspectives, step up and show what's next — optimistic, upbeat people with a can-do attitude, a hunger to learn and grow, and a genuine love for collaboration. In practice this means:
 


* 3+ years of experience in product design, with a strong focus on native mobile app design (iOS and/or Android).
* Deep knowledge of mobile design patterns, platform guidelines (Apple HIG, Material Design), and mobile interaction paradigms including gestures, haptics, and adaptive layouts.
* Proven experience designing and shipping mobile apps, with the ability to demonstrate impact on user engagement and satisfaction.
* Experience working with scrum teams and tools like Jira and Confluence, and strong skills in Figma including mobile prototyping and handoff workflows.
* Experience in UI design and micro-interactions, with a particular strength in mobile motion design and transitions.
* Strong verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to present your design thinking clearly to audiences with varying levels of design understanding.
* Deep empathy for users and a passion for representing their needs and motivations to diverse stakeholder groups, especially in mobile-first and on-the-go contexts.
* Creative problem-solving skills and a thoughtful, proactive mindset in identifying opportunities for improving the user experience.
* An optimistic, collaborative personality — you bring warmth and energy to the team, value diverse perspectives, and believe the best work happens when people support each other.
* Experience in B2B SaaS and/or the Payments sector is a nice-to-have — what we care about most is your mobile design craft. Industry knowledge you'll learn on the job.
* You have an online portfolio showcasing mobile app design work, which you can share upon application.
* You are available to work full time.



  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Worldline you'll get the chance to be at the heart of the global payments technology industry and shape how the world pays and gets paid. On top of that, you will also:
 


* Work with inspiring colleagues in a supportive, inclusive environment and be empowered to learn, grow and accelerate your career.
* Health contribution
* Health check – Every 2:nd year
* Private Health Care Insurance (EuroAccident)
* Parental pay and 12 months pension during parental leave
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard
* Benefit portal with many great offers, plus more.



  





**Shape the evolution** 




 We are pushing towards the next frontiers of payments technology, and we look for talented people to join our journey. People with passion, a can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. Here you'll work with ambitious colleagues from around the world, take on unique challenges as a team, and make a real impact on society. And with our empowering culture, strong technology and extensive training opportunities, we help you accelerate your career. Wherever you decide to go. Join our global team of over 18,000 innovators across 40+ countries, and shape a tomorrow that is yours to own.
 



  





 Learn more about life at Worldline at jobs.worldline.com
 



  





 We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or any applicable legally protected characteristics.

title: Lead UX/UI Designer
description:
**This is Worldline** 




 Help shape the future of how the world pays and gets paid. At Worldline, we enable businesses of all sizes to grow quickly, simply, and securely. As a leader in the payments industry, we power millions of companies across five continents. Just as we drive our customers' growth, we support our people in building their careers. Together, we shape the evolution of payments.
 



  





 If you're passionate about leading design that makes a real difference and ready to help shape the direction of a talented, growing design team, this is your chance. Join us and help redefine how the world pays.
 



  





**The opportunity** 




 Design at Worldline is going through an exciting transformation. We're raising the bar — fast. We're building a world-class design system, embedding user-centered thinking deeper into how products are shaped, and growing a diverse, talented team that is genuinely changing how the organisation approaches design. The momentum is real, the ambition is high, and the impact is visible. This is a rare moment: the kind where a strong design leader can help define what great looks like for years to come.
 



  





 As Lead Product Designer in the Worldline Design team, you will be a driving force behind design quality, strategic direction, and team development. You will lead by example — setting the bar for craft and user advocacy — while coaching and mentoring fellow designers to grow their skills and confidence. Your mission is to connect business strategy with user needs, translating complex product challenges into coherent, intuitive experiences across our portfolio. You will work closely with product management, engineering leads, and senior stakeholders to ensure design has a strong voice at the table, and you will help shape the practices and culture of our design organisation.
 



  





 This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden.
 



  





**Day-to-day responsibilities** 



* Lead the design direction for key product areas, ensuring a coherent and high-quality user experience across complex payment solutions.
* Mentor and coach product designers, fostering a culture of continuous improvement, craft excellence, and constructive, supportive design critique.
* Partner with product managers, engineering leads, and business stakeholders to define product strategy and roadmaps, ensuring user needs are represented from the start.
* Guide design projects from discovery through delivery — setting direction, navigating trade-offs thoughtfully, and ensuring design intent is preserved in implementation.
* Facilitate design workshops, design sprints, and cross-team alignment sessions that bring out the best thinking in everyone involved.
* Collaborate with our Design System experts to champion consistency, scalability, and quality across products.
* Raise the bar on user research integration — ensuring designs are grounded in evidence and validated with real users.
* Actively explore opportunities for our business and the field of UX with generative and Agentic AI.
* Stay ahead of industry trends and bring strategic perspective on how emerging patterns and technologies can elevate our products.
* Contribute to the growth and culture of the Worldline Design team — championing an inclusive, supportive environment where diverse perspectives make the work stronger.



  





**Who are we looking for** 




 We look for talented, thoughtful design leaders who care about their craft and the people around them. People who bring fresh perspectives, step up and show what's next — optimistic, upbeat people with a can-do attitude, a hunger to learn and grow, and a genuine love for collaboration. In practice this means:
 


* 7+ years of experience in product design, with at least 2 years in a lead, senior, or principal design role where you have guided other designers.
* A strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end product design leadership across complex, multi-stakeholder projects.
* Proven ability to mentor and develop designers, providing thoughtful, actionable feedback and fostering professional growth and confidence.
* Experience partnering with product management and engineering at a strategic level to shape product direction.
* Sophisticated skills in Figma and other design tools, with the ability to establish and promote best practices within a team.
* Strong understanding of user research methodologies and the ability to integrate insights into the design process at the right moments.
* Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders and articulate design rationale clearly.
* Deep empathy for users and a passion for representing their needs and motivations — even when business pressures push in other directions.
* A systems-thinking mindset, comfortable navigating complexity, ambiguity, and trade-offs across a broad product portfolio.
* An optimistic, collaborative personality — you lead with warmth, value diverse perspectives, lift others up, and believe the best work happens when people support each other.
* Experience in B2B SaaS and/or the Payments sector is a nice-to-have — exceptional design leadership is what matters most, and industry knowledge you'll pick up on the job.
* You have an online portfolio showcasing your work and design leadership, which you can share upon application.
* You are available to work full time.



  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Worldline you'll get the chance to be at the heart of the global payments technology industry and shape how the world pays and gets paid. On top of that, you will also:
 


* Work with inspiring colleagues in a supportive, inclusive environment and be empowered to learn, grow and accelerate your career.
* Health contribution
* Health check – Every 2:nd year
* Private Health Care Insurance (EuroAccident)
* Parental pay and 12 months pension during parental leave
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard
* Benefit portal with many great offers, plus more.



  





**Shape the evolution** 




 We are pushing towards the next frontiers of payments technology, and we look for talented people to join our journey. People with passion, a can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. Here you'll work with ambitious colleagues from around the world, take on unique challenges as a team, and make a real impact on society. And with our empowering culture, strong technology and extensive training opportunities, we help you accelerate your career. Wherever you decide to go. Join our global team of over 18,000 innovators across 40+ countries, and shape a tomorrow that is yours to own.
 



  





 Learn more about life at Worldline at jobs.worldline.com
 



  





 We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or any applicable legally protected characteristics.

title: Creative Designer
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 As a Creative Designer at Air Apps, you will play a crucial role in shaping our brand identity and delivering visually engaging designs across multiple platforms. You will collaborate with various teams to ensure a cohesive and compelling user experience, working on digital ads, social media content, landing pages, email campaigns, and print materials.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**Responsibilities**
* Develop and maintain a consistent visual identity that aligns with Air Apps’ brand guidelines.
* Create engaging graphics, illustrations, infographics, and layouts for web, social media, email campaigns, and print materials.
* Collaborate with Marketing, Product, and Content teams to translate requirements into compelling visual solutions.
* Iterate on designs based on feedback from stakeholders and refine assets using user insights and performance metrics.
* Ensure high-quality output and conduct design quality assurance across various channels.
* Utilize industry-standard design software (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, Figma) to create and deliver assets in multiple formats.


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, or a related field.
* Around 3 years of experience creating assets for digital marketing campaigns, social media, and web.
* Strong portfolio showcasing a range of design projects and styles.
* Knowledge of typography, color theory, layout, and design best practices.
* Proficiency in industry-standard design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and Figma.
* Strong communication, time-management, and attention-to-detail skills.
* Ability to work in a fast-paced, remote environment and collaborate effectively across teams.


 What benefits are we offering?
   

  

* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


 Diversity & Inclusion
   

  

 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

 Application Disclaimer
   

  

 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

title: Web Developer Intern
description:
p>A Swedish financial technology group is expanding its operational footprint, requiring robust digital tools to manage their evolving financial data. In this role, you will support the development of these web-based tools while gaining exposure to our rigorous approach to financial advisory.
   

**About Blue Oak Consulting**
 Blue Oak Consulting provides independent advisory on pricing, portfolio economics, capital allocation, and acquisition assumptions. We are not a traditional consulting firm focused on broad transformation programs or high level strategy decks. Instead, we interrogate specific commercial decisions where the financial stakes are high and the existing narrative is not sufficient. Our team looks past corporate language and P and L complexity to identify where value is leaking or capital is trapped. We work across sectors like software, manufacturing, and healthcare to test whether the assumptions behind a growth plan or an acquisition actually hold under pressure. We are a small, principal led firm where the people who do the work are the ones accountable for the results. We do not take transaction contingent fees, which allows us to be completely honest about whether the economics of a decision make sense. Our goal is to provide clarity through financial discipline and a healthy amount of skepticism. We stay through the execution phase because decisions that look sound in theory often fail when they meet real world friction.
   

  

**The Role**
 As a Web Developer Intern, you will operate as a member of a fully remote team, focusing on the technical side of our advisory work. The environment is one of technical discipline and intellectual honesty, where code is expected to be as reliable as the financial models it houses. You will spend your time translating complex economic requirements into straightforward web interfaces that provide our clients with a clear view of their portfolio performance. This position is a chance to see how data visualization and web tools function in the context of high stakes financial decision making.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing** 
* Assisting in the development and maintenance of internal web applications used for financial modeling.
* Writing clean and functional HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to update client facing data dashboards.
* Helping to build out new features for our reporting tools that track fintech sector metrics.
* Participating in code reviews to ensure all web elements align with our standards for accuracy.
* Collaborating with our analysts to understand how back end data should be displayed for the best user experience.
* Testing web tools across different browsers to ensure consistent performance for our remote team and clients.
* Documenting technical components and workflows to help maintain our internal knowledge base.
* Troubleshooting basic front end issues and implementing fixes to improve site navigation.
  

  


**What You Bring** 
* A basic understanding of front end web technologies, including HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.
* Foundational knowledge of web design principles, specifically an interest in data visualization.
* A pursuing or recently completed degree in Computer Science, Web Development, or a related technical field.
* Comfort working in a fully remote environment where clear written communication is vital.
* A methodical approach to solving problems and a high level of attention to detail.
* Curiosity about how businesses operate and a willingness to learn about financial concepts.
* The ability to follow technical instructions closely and ask clarifying questions when needed.
  

  


**What We Offer** 
* The ability to work from anywhere in this fully remote position.
* Direct access to senior principals who value technical logic and evidence based results.
* Practical experience working on projects for a major international fintech group.
* A professional environment that prioritizes discipline and clarity over corporate jargon.
* Exposure to the intersection of finance and technology in a real world setting.
* A paid internship with a competitive and attractive rate for the current market.
  

  


 At Blue Oak Consulting, we believe that simple, functional technology is the best way to present complicated financial truths. We do not hide behind complex language, and we expect our code to follow the same principle of transparency and functionality. This role is a chance to learn how to build technology that serves a clear purpose: helping businesses make better, more disciplined decisions through data clarity.

title: Product Manager
description:
Our customers rely on our platform to manage complex workflows, collaborate with partners, and deliver great service. When the product gets in their way, productivity suffers. When it works seamlessly, they can focus on what matters most.
 



  





 We're looking for a Product Manager who puts customers at the centre of every decision and enjoys solving meaningful problems through simple, impactful products.
 



  





**What you'll do** 



* Own the roadmap for your product area, from discovery through delivery.
* Work closely with customers to understand their needs and turn insights into product decisions.
* Partner with design and engineering to deliver intuitive, high-quality experiences.
* Balance customer value, business goals, and technical considerations when prioritizing work.
* Use data, customer feedback, and experimentation to continuously improve the product.
* Explore practical ways AI can improve both the product and internal ways of working.
* Align stakeholders across the business through clear communication and thoughtful prioritization.



  





**What you'll bring** 



* 3+ years of Product Management experience with ownership of a product area or feature set.
* Ability to bring structure to ambiguous problems and make steady progress.
* Strong product sense with a focus on usability and customer experience.
* Experience using data and customer insights to guide decisions.
* Curiosity about AI and emerging technologies.
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to work cross-functionally.



  





 Experience with B2B SaaS, marketplace products, or other workflow-intensive software environments is an advantage.

title: Design Engineer
description:
Build something monumental for Healthcare!
   

  

 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You care deeply about how products feel. Not just how they work, but how they
 *look, respond, and guide users* 
 . At your previous role, you were probably the person who noticed the small details others missed, the awkward interaction, the unnecessary friction, the moment where the product could have been better.
   

  

 At Tandem, you’ll bring that mindset to healthcare, a space where most software still feels slow, clunky, and frustrating to use. You’ll help us change that!
   

  

 As a Design Engineer, you’ll work closely with designers, product, and other engineers to build experiences that are fast, intuitive, and genuinely enjoyable to use. Your core strength lies in frontend development, crafting high-quality user interfaces - while still being comfortable working across the stack when needed. You’ll take ideas from rough concepts to polished features in production, with a strong sense of ownership over both the technical implementation and the final user experience.
   

  

 This is a role for someone who moves quickly, has strong product taste, and cares about getting the details right - because those details are what make a product feel great.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Own and ship user-facing features end-to-end, from early concept to polished production experience.
* Work closely with designers to translate ideas into high-quality, interactive UI, and iterate quickly based on feedback.
* Build and maintain fast, responsive, and scalable frontend applications, primarily using modern web technologies.
* Contribute across the stack when needed - integrating APIs, shaping data flows, and ensuring a smooth end-to-end experience.
* Continuously refine and improve the product by identifying friction, inconsistencies, and opportunities for better UX.
* Take ownership of UI quality, performance, and consistency across the product.
* Use the best modern tools (including AI-assisted development) to ship high-quality features quickly.


 What You Bring
   

  

* Strong experience building modern frontend applications with TypeScript and React.
* A clear strength and interest in frontend development - you care deeply about UI, performance, and user experience.
* A sharp eye for design, interaction, and detail, you care about spacing, motion, responsiveness, and polish.
* A product mindset - you think in terms of user outcomes, not just technical tasks.
* Ability to move quickly from idea to implementation, with a high degree of ownership.
* Comfort working across the stack when needed (e.g. APIs, backend integrations).
* Experience shipping production systems and understanding how to balance speed with quality.
* Strong collaboration skills - you enjoy working closely with designers and other engineers.


 Bonus Points
   

  

* Experience in the healthcare industry or with health-related applications.
* Experience working in design-heavy or product-led teams.
* Familiarity with tools like Figma and ability to collaborate closely on design systems.
* Experience building LLM-powered interfaces or AI-driven products.


 Location
   

  

 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

 How to Apply
   

  

 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

**Our Interview Process Consists Of 4 Stages**
* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interviews with some of our Engineering team
* System Design Interview at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch and a Product focused System Design interview to experience our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.
* Follow-up interview with our CTO & Co-Founder


 We're currently in the middle of vacation season, which means it may take us a little longer to review applications and get back to you. Thanks for your patience - we're looking forward to reading your application!
   

  

**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 30 days/year of paid vacation
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag) plus an additional 6,000 SEK yearly to spend on other health related initiatives
* Generous Parental leave top-up for new parents
* Private Medical Insurance to stay healthy
* Mental health support through our partner Mindler
* Pension program
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


*We review our benefits packages on a regular basis and might modify our benefits from time to time.*
 Culture at Tandem
   

  

 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*

title: Customer Support Agent
description:
**We are Europe’s leading provider of employee benefits technology.**
 Millions of employers and staff use Epassi’s digital and mobile solutions to seamlessly redeem employee benefits across our unrivalled merchant network.
   

  

 Epassi - Wellbeing that works!
   

  

 Are you interested in joining one of Europe’s fastest-growing companies? Do you want to impact the wellbeing of millions of users?
   

  

 If yes, join us to make an impact in a fast-paced and dynamic environment where you’ll play a key role in delivering an exceptional support experience that keeps our users at the heart of everything we do.
   

  

 Epassi’s purpose is to boost everyday well-being. We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions across Europe, established in Finland in 2007. Since then, we have consistently grown, diversifying our products and expanding into new markets. Epassi has been recognised by the Financial Times as one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe on multiple occasions.
   

  

**We are currently hiring a Customer Support Agent to join our team in Stockholm.**
**Your Mission at Epassi**
 As a Customer Support Agent, you will be the face of Epassi - supporting and interacting with our key stakeholders: end-users, clients, and partners. You will resolve support queries through phone, email and chat, guiding customers through the product journey with technical know-how and genuine care. This is a fixed-term position starting as soon as possible and concluding on 31 January 2027, with the possibility of extension depending on performance and company needs.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Will Be**
* Resolving customer support cases through phone, email and chat efficiently and professionally, delivering best-in-class service
* Guiding stakeholders from a technical and product perspective through the user journey
* Collaborating with colleagues and other teams to find the best solutions
* Identifying recurring issues and feeding back findings to drive product development
* Keeping up to date with new and changing product features


**What You’ll Be Doing (Daily)**
* Monitoring and responding to support tickets, chat and phone channels
* Logging, categorising and updating support cases accurately
* Troubleshooting issues with the Epassi application and platform, escalating where needed
* Staying informed on known platform issues and upcoming product releases
* Contributing to stand-ups and support team meetings


**You’ll Be Successful in This Role If You**
* Easily grasp new information and operating systems
* Are an easy-going team player with a can-do attitude, who also works well independently
* Excel in communication, provide excellent service, and have a strong eye for detail
* Are structured and effective in how you work


**What You Should Bring**
* A natural curiosity and a technical mindset - you enjoy figuring out how things work
* Confidence in conversation and strong active listening skills
* Excellent attention to detail and accuracy
* An ability to stay organised and calm under pressure in a fast-paced environment
* A genuinely customer-centric attitude - you care about the experience you deliver
* A collaborative, positive approach and willingness to support the wider team
* 1–2 years of experience in customer service or a relevant field is a merit
* Knowledge of Zendesk is a plus


**Your Language Profile**
* Swedish - professional working proficiency (essential)
* English - professional working proficiency (essential)


**Why You Should Join Us**
* Be part of an ambitious growth journey and a global community of driven, bold, entrepreneurial Epassians in a company that is constantly evolving
* Work with a clear purpose - boosting everyday wellbeing - and create a meaningful, positive impact on both individuals and society
* Thrive in an open and inclusive culture with low hierarchy, where relationships are built on respect, camaraderie, and shared successes
* Benefit from a great package including private health care, lunch benefit, wellbeing benefit, and snacks at the office
* Work on a hybrid basis - 3 days in the office each week (5 days per week during onboarding)
* Join a fast-growing tech company with a strong financial foundation and real opportunities to grow

title: ServiceDesk Analyst
description:
**Om jobbet**
 Är du i början av din IT-karriär – eller redo att ta nästa steg?
   

  

 Hos oss på DataCom får du en roll där du är med och gör verklig skillnad i våra kunders vardag.
   

  

 Som ServiceDesk Analyst är du första kontaktpunkten för våra användare och kunder. Du tar emot ärenden via telefon, mail, chatt och portal, felsöker och löser problem, och ser till att varje ärende hanteras professionellt hela vägen från start till lösning.
   

  

 Du jobbar nära både kollegor och andra funktioner inom DataCom och bidrar till att skapa en stabil, effektiv och kundfokuserad supportleverans. Vi tror på värdet av att arbeta tillsammans på plats och söker en medarbetare som vill vara en del av teamet på kontoret varje dag.
   

  

**Din vardag hos oss**
 I rollen kommer du bland annat att:
   

  

* Hantera inkommande supportärenden, registrera och prioritera i vårt ärendehanteringssystem
* Felsöka och lösa incidenter och service requests
* Kommunicera tydligt och professionellt med användare genom hela ärendets livscykel
* Eskalera ärenden vid behov till rätt funktion
* Arbeta proaktivt för att minska återkommande problem och förbättra användarupplevelsen
* Dokumentera lösningar och bidra till vår kunskapsdatabas
* Samarbeta nära teamet och bidra till en positiv och professionell teamkultur


**Vi letar efter dig som vill utvecklas inom IT och trivs i en roll med mycket kundkontakt och högt tempo** 
 .
   

  

 Vi tror att du:
   

  

* Har arbetat cirka 1–2 år inom IT-support eller ServiceDesk med fokus på användarsupport, felsökning av Windows och Microsoft 365, konto och behörighetshantering i Active Directory/ Entra ID samt grundläggande nätverks och klientadministration.
* Van att arbeta strukturerat i ärendehanteringssystem
* Du är serviceinriktad, kommunikativ och tycker om att arbeta i en roll där du kombinerar teknik med användarstöd.
* Kommunicerar tydligt och professionellt, både på svenska och engelska
* Har förmågan att prioritera och arbeta i ett högt tempo
* Är strukturerad och tar ansvar för ditt arbete


**Därför ska du söka till DataCom**
 På DataCom får du möjlighet att utvecklas i ett bolag som tar ansvar, arbetar långsiktigt och växer tillsammans med sina kunder. Vi kombinerar teknik med närvaro, struktur med flexibilitet och höga krav med ett varmt samarbete i vardagen.
   

  

 Hos oss handlar potential inte bara om vad du redan kan, utan vart du vill. Det viktigaste du tar med dig till DataCom är inte nödvändigtvis teknisk spets, utan viljan att lära, bidra och utvecklas tillsammans med andra. Vi tror att mod, driv och nyfikenhet är minst lika viktiga som erfarenhet.

title: Analytics Engineer
description:
**TL;DR** 
 - We are looking for analytics engineers to own the semantic and modeling layer of our warehouse by transforming raw data into well-defined, trustworthy datasets and metrics. You are obsessed with analytical consistency, designing impactful metrics, and building strong foundations for self-service analytics.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What we are looking for:**
* Expertise with SQL, dbt, SQLMesh or similar tools (data modeling, testing, macros, docs)
* Experience with data warehousing concepts, cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks) and BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Hex, Metabase, etc)
* Understanding of dimensional modeling, data contracts, and metrics/semantical layers
* Familiarity with modern ELT and orchestration workflows (Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, etc)
* Strong business acumen and ability to translate domain logic into scalable data structures


**What you will do:**
* Build and maintain data models, following modular, tested, and version-controlled practices
* Partner with domain teams to understand business logic and codify it into reusable models and metrics
* Define and document key metrics and data contracts across domains
* Collaborate with Data Platform Engineers to optimize query performance and warehouse cost
* Automate and maintain data documentation, lineage, and governance standards
* Develop guidelines for analytics development, data modeling and structure conventions


**Our tech stack**
 We're building with tools that both humans and AI love:
   

  

* Frontend: React
* Backend: Golang and Rust
* Cloud: Cloudflare, GCP, AWS, Many LLM providers
* DevOps & Tooling: Github Actions, Grafana, OTEL, infrastructure-as-code (Terraform)


 And always on the lookout for what's next!
   

  

**How We Hire**
* Fill in a short form then jump on an intro call with recruiter.
* Complete the general programming exercise.
* Show us how you approach problems during several technical interviews.
* Tell us about your most impressive project.


**About Your Application**
* Please submit your application in English - it’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join
* We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested please apply through our careers portal

title: Juicer - (Stockholm West)
description:
**WHY WORK AT JOE & THE JUICE**
 JOE & THE JUICE is a people-centric food and beverage company built around culture, not just juice, sandwiches, coffee, and much more. When joining us, you step into a high-energy community where the music is loud, the bar is your stage, and your personality matters as much as your skills.
   

  

 We believe in four simple virtues that shape everything we do:
   

  

* Positive Attitude
* Inclusion
* Social Ties
* Growth


 For us, this means that when you choose a cup-half-full mindset, welcome people from every background, build real friendships across the bar by creating great guest experiences, and use every shift as a chance to learn something new — you become more than just a Juicer; like us, you continue to search and find purpose and meaning in what you do.
   

  

 From your first day, you’ll be part of an international network of Juicers who support each other, celebrate wins together, and push each other to grow. You will learn by doing, move through a clear Moneyball career path, and be part of a company where most leaders have earned their stripes behind the bar (fun fact: over 75% of our employees started as Juicers!).
   

  

 If you love fast-paced service, music, people, and the idea of turning a job into a lifestyle, JOE can become a place to build skills, friendships, and a career that can take you across cities, countries, and roles.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Deliver exceptional guest experiences with enthusiasm and attentiveness.
* Ensure product quality by following the product manual for consistency and excellence.
* Maintain hygiene standards by adhering to company cleanliness policies and guidelines.


**KEY EXPECTATIONS**
* Foster a positive work environment with a "Happy to be here" attitude.
* Demonstrate dedication and a high work ethic to uphold Joe & The Juice’s standards.
* Embrace a humble and honest approach to build trust and collaboration within the team.
* Adhere to punctual attendance, dress code, and high personal hygiene standards.
* Possess knowledge of and follow all company policies and procedures.


 We expect our Juicers to be engaged, open-minded, and to maintain a positive attitude in all that they do. While hard work and dedication are essential, we also encourage our team to have fun and enjoy each other's and our guests' company. We offer a work environment with great opportunities for development and progression, expecting consistent performance in return.
   

  

**WHY WORK WITH US**
* Great Company Culture: Be part of a team that values positive attitudes, inclusivity, and social ties. Enjoy a packed social calendar with various events that foster camaraderie and create lasting memories.
* Leadership & Educational Programs: Access continuous learning and growth opportunities through our comprehensive internal programs. Participate in workshops, training sessions, and mentorship programs designed to enhance your professional skills and leadership capabilities.
* Travel & Development Opportunities: Experience diverse cultures and locations while developing your skills and career. Take advantage of our exchange programs, international assignments, and career advancement opportunities that allow you to grow within the company and explore new horizons.


 By joining Joe & the Juice, you are contributing to a healthier world, one juice at a time. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds and encourage diversity in our workplace. Together, we can create memorable guest experiences and build a vibrant, successful community.
   

  

**Ready to embark on this exciting journey? Apply today and become a part of Joe & The Juice!**
**About Joe & The Juice**
 JOE & THE JUICE is a healthy fast-casual concept founded in Copenhagen in 2002; since then, we have grown to more than 500 locations across 20 countries and now employ more than 4,500 people worldwide. From day one, our ambition has been to prove that there should be no trade-off between taste and health.
   

  

 We serve high-quality, freshly prepared juices, shakes, coffee, and sandwiches in relaxed, contemporary concept cafés that combine convenience with distinct urban atmospheres. JOE & THE JUICE goes beyond products towards the full experience around the bar, creating a consistent universe for guests and Juicers in all our locations.
   

  

 We invest heavily in internal development through our Moneyball career system and a culture where most leaders, including C-suite, VPs, and directors, have started behind the bar. We continue to evolve our people-centric and sustainability agenda that is part of our Nordic heritage and our commitment to a better future.

title: Android Engineer I - Subscriptions
description:
The Subscriptions R&D team oversees the entire user journey on Spotify and ensures we engage with people in innovative ways, every step of the way. Our team grows Spotify’s audience by finding future listeners around the world and delivering the right value to them, at the right time. With research, product development, product design, engineering, and marketing all collaborating in one organization, we’re able to quickly create meaningful features and services for millions of people around the world, resulting in joyful, long-lasting relationships with Spotify.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Design, build, and evolve mobile experiences across some of Spotify’s most visible consumer surfaces.
* Contribute to the architecture and development of reusable mobile foundations, frameworks, and platform capabilities.
* Support and develop technical initiatives that improve scalability, performance, reliability, and developer experience across the Spotify app.
* Collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, designers, and platform teams to shape product direction and technical strategy.
* Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and drive quality through testing practices, observability, and operational excellence.
* Participate in experimentation and prototyping efforts tied to future-facing content and listening experiences.
* Explore modern engineering workflows, including AI-assisted development tools and emerging mobile technologies.
* Balance short-term product needs with long-term platform sustainability and technical health.
* Learn, grow, and contribute to Spotify's engineering culture through collaboration, knowledge sharing, and curiosity.


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience developing Android applications using Kotlin.
* You are aware of how to build maintainable, scalable, reusable and well-tested software systems.
* You have experience collaborating with engineers and colleagues across disciplines.
* You care about code quality, testing, documentation, and long-term maintainability.
* You demonstrate curiosity, ownership, and a willingness to step outside your immediate area of expertise when needed.
* You are curious about using modern development tooling, including AI-assisted coding solutions.
* You bring a growth mindset and continuously look for opportunities to learn, improve systems, products, and team practices.
* You might have familiarity with SQL, analytics systems, or data engineering concepts.


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! For this role, you can be within the EMEA region as long as we have a work location (excluding France due to on-call restrictions).
* This team operates within the Central European and GMT time zone for collaboration.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Design System Manager
description:
**This is Worldline** 




 Help shape the future of how the world pays and gets paid. At Worldline, we enable businesses of all sizes to grow quickly, simply, and securely. As a leader in the payments industry, we power millions of companies across five continents. Just as we drive our customers' growth, we support our people in building their careers. Together, we shape the evolution of payments.
 



 If you're passionate about building the foundations that make great design scalable and consistent — and you want to do it alongside a talented team where design is accelerating fast — this is your chance. Join us and help redefine how the world pays.
 



  





**The opportunity** 




 Design at Worldline is going through an exciting transformation. We're raising the bar — fast. At the centre of that transformation is our Design System: a world-class foundation we're building with talented people who care deeply about craft, consistency, and scale. It's already creating significant impact across our product portfolio, and we're just getting started. The ambition is high, the team is strong and growing more diverse every day, and there's a real sense that we're shaking things up.
 



  





 As our Design System Manager, you will own the strategy, governance, and evolution of the Worldline Design System — the shared foundation that enables our product teams to deliver consistent, high-quality experiences at scale. Coming from a design background, you understand the craft and the "why" behind every component; with a strong affinity for frontend development, you can bridge the gap between design decisions and technical implementation. Your mission is to make the Design System a product in its own right — one that serves designers and developers alike, accelerates delivery, and raises the quality bar across our entire product portfolio. You will lead cross-functional collaboration, align stakeholders, and build the processes that keep the system healthy and evolving.
 



  





 This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden.
 



  





**Day-to-day responsibilities** 



* Own the vision, roadmap, and governance of the Worldline Design System, treating it as a product with clear goals, priorities, and success metrics.
* Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of design system components — from identifying needs and defining patterns, through design and documentation, to handoff and adoption tracking.
* Bridge the gap between design and frontend engineering, ensuring components are both designerly and technically sound — working closely with developers who implement the system in code.
* Establish and maintain contribution guidelines, quality standards, and governance processes so the system scales sustainably.
* Collaborate closely with product designers across teams to understand their needs, gather feedback, and ensure the system evolves to support real product challenges.
* Champion design tokens, accessibility standards, and responsive patterns to ensure consistency across platforms and products.
* Nurture adoption of the Design System across product teams, providing training, documentation, and hands-on support to designers and developers.
* Stay ahead of design system trends, tooling innovations, and frontend technologies to continuously improve how we build and maintain our system.
* Actively explore opportunities for our business and the field of design systems with generative and Agentic AI — from automated documentation to intelligent component suggestions.
* Advocate for the Design System as a strategic asset within the organisation, communicating its value to senior stakeholders and product leadership.
* Become a key member of the Worldline Design team, shaping our design infrastructure and contributing to our culture of consistency, craft, and mutual support.



  





**Who are we looking for** 




 We look for talented, thoughtful people who care about their craft and the people they serve. People who bring fresh perspectives, step up and show what's next — optimistic, upbeat people with a can-do attitude, a hunger to learn and grow, and a genuine love for collaboration. In practice this means:
 


* 5+ years of experience in product design or design systems, with at least 2 years of hands-on experience building, maintaining, or managing a design system.
* A design background with a strong understanding of UI design principles, component architecture, design patterns, and visual consistency.
* Frontend affinity — you may not write production code daily, but you understand HTML/CSS, component frameworks (e.g., React), design tokens, and how design translates to implementation. Hands-on frontend experience is a strong plus.
* Proven experience managing a design system as a product — including roadmapping, prioritisation, stakeholder management, and adoption measurement.
* Expert-level Figma skills, including component architecture, variants, variables, auto-layout, and library management.
* Strong understanding of accessibility standards (WCAG) and how they apply to design system components.
* Experience creating and maintaining design system documentation that serves both designers and developers.
* Excellent verbal and written English communication skills, with the ability to align diverse stakeholders — from designers and developers to product managers and leadership.
* A collaborative and service-oriented mindset — you see the design system as an enabler for others and find satisfaction in making your colleagues more effective.
* A systems-thinking mindset with a keen eye for detail and a passion for order, scalability, and maintainability.
* An optimistic, warm personality — you bring positive energy to your collaborations, value diverse perspectives, and believe great systems are built by great teams supporting each other.
* Industry experience in payments or B2B SaaS is a nice-to-have — what matters most is your design system expertise and design craft. Domain knowledge you'll learn on the job.
* You have an online portfolio or case study showcasing your design system work, which you can share upon application.
* You are available to work full time.



  





**Perks & Benefits** 




 At Worldline you'll get the chance to be at the heart of the global payments technology industry and shape how the world pays and gets paid. On top of that, you will also:
 


* Work with inspiring colleagues in a supportive, inclusive environment and be empowered to learn, grow and accelerate your career.
* Health contribution
* Health check – Every 2:nd year
* Private Health Care Insurance (EuroAccident)
* Parental pay and 12 months pension during parental leave
* Opportunity to hybrid working according to company standard
* Benefit portal with many great offers, plus more.



  





**Shape the evolution** 




 We are pushing towards the next frontiers of payments technology, and we look for talented people to join our journey. People with passion, a can-do attitude and a hunger to learn and grow. Here you'll work with ambitious colleagues from around the world, take on unique challenges as a team, and make a real impact on society. And with our empowering culture, strong technology and extensive training opportunities, we help you accelerate your career. Wherever you decide to go. Join our global team of over 18,000 innovators across 40+ countries, and shape a tomorrow that is yours to own.
 



  





 Learn more about life at Worldline at jobs.worldline.com
 



  





 We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, colour, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as an individual with a disability, or any applicable legally protected characteristics.

title: IT Security Specialist
description:
**Lead and develop IT security at Bonava in Stockholm. Work with Azure, Microsoft 365 and cybersecurity in a cloud-first environment.**



At Bonava, we are not only building houses, we are also creating homes and neighborhoods where people live their lives. To keep our digital world safe, we are now looking for an IT Security Specialist to lead and develop our cybersecurity landscape.



This is a broad role where you will combine strategic vision with hands-on expertise. You will work closely with colleagues across infrastructure, governance and operations to strengthen our security posture, drive continuous improvement and ensure our cloud-first environment remains secure, compliant and resilient.



If you are motivated by taking ownership, influencing security strategies and working with modern Microsoft technologies in a business-critical environment, this is an opportunity to make a real impact across the organization.


**About the job**  

At Bonava, you will have the opportunity to shape how we protect our systems, services and data moving forward. You will join an organization where security is an important enabler for the business and where your expertise will influence both strategy and operations.


You will have the opportunity to:


* Take overall responsibility for IT security across Bonava
* Work in a cloud-first environment built primarily on Microsoft Azure
* Influence and develop security frameworks, processes and ways of working
* Combine strategic security initiatives with hands-on technical work
* Collaborate with stakeholders across IT and the wider business
* Enjoy a flexible hybrid working model from our central Stockholm office



As IT Security Specialist, you will take overall responsibility for IT security at Bonava. This is a broad role where you will work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders to continuously strengthen the organization’s security posture.



You will:


* Strengthen and maintain security across Bonava’s systems and data
* Monitor the threat landscape and initiate preventive measures
* Manage and develop security infrastructure
* Educate users and promote a strong security culture across the organization
* Drive improvement initiatives related to security and compliance
* Provide informed guidance to management on security risks, incidents and strategic priorities to enhance the organization’s security posture

You will be part of the IT Infrastructure and Compliance team, working alongside colleagues within infrastructure, governance and operations.



Bonava operates with a cloud-first IT strategy, primarily leveraging Microsoft Azure. While the focus is on modern cloud-based solutions, the environment remains hybrid, with certain systems and services still managed on- premises.


The position is based in central Stockholm and offers a hybrid work model with flexibility to work from home.


Work with great people


**Anders Bengtsson**
IT Service Lead


"Bonava has the perfect size and culture. We are large enough to offer a wide range of interesting topics and skilled colleagues, yet small enough for each individual to make a real impact. What I do matters, and that is a great feeling."
**Yulia Noskova**
Environment and Climate Action Manager


"Bonava is a perfect place for a curious mind to learn, explore new matters, and push own boundaries. With all the trust and ownership that I’m given, I feel I can truly make a change and contribute."
**Emma Staffansson**
Facility Manager


"Bonava has an including and open culture, you feel it from the start. I am very grateful for working in a company where we all support each other both within and across teams."
Skills & Requirements


We believe you will thrive in this role if you have a few years of experience within IT Security or Infrastructure and enjoy working in an environment where strategic thinking and hands-on problem-solving go hand in hand.


You also have:


* Strong knowledge of Microsoft 365 Security tools, like Defender, Intune and Sentinel
* Hands-on experience with Microsoft Defender, Intune and automation
* Understanding of Azure environments and hybrid setups
* Understanding of network security principles
* Experience working with enterprise network environments
* Skills in scripting, preferably PowerShell or Python
* A proactive, structured and responsible approach
* Excellent communication skills in English

As a person, you are collaborative and take initiative to move your work forward. You are a clear and confident communicator who builds trust with stakeholders across the organization. You exercise good judgement, stay up to date with developments in the cybersecurity landscape and enjoy finding practical solutions to complex challenges.


It is considered a plus if you have experience working with governance, risk and compliance frameworks, as well as log management and analysis. Swedish language skills are also considered an advantage.


Great Place to Work


Engaged Employees


Global Environment


Hybrid Workplace


About the company


Bonava is a leading residential developer in Europe with the purpose to create happy neighbourhoods for the many. With its 900 employees, Bonava develops residential housing in Germany, Sweden, Finland, Latvia, Estonia and Lithuania. To date, the company has built about 50,000 homes and reported net sales of approximately EUR 700 million in 2025. Bonava’s shares and green bond are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm.
